mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increase in the industrial production index signals expanding manufacturing output, which positively predicts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through higher corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day S&P 500 returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not positive in more than 50% of out-of-sample observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating household income and demand contraction, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 55% of instances when the trigger fires during a 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates while the federal funds rate remains comparatively low, negative real interest rates stimulate borrowing and risk-taking, jointly predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with inflationary pressure
failure_condition: The interaction term of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to generate a statistically significant positive coefficient (p > 0.05) in a pooled regression predicting 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth positively predicts S&P 500 forward returns when the economy is operating near full employment, because output gains translate into margin expansion rather than just hiring; this mechanism is absent when slack is high.
failure_condition: During periods when UNRATE is below 5.0%, positive GDPC1 surprises fail to produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of quarterly observations over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in near-term earnings resilience.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS fail to coincide with positive S&P 500 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,400 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment and downstream consumption, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of instances when HOUST is below 1400 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in industrial production and retail sales indicate a broad-based expansion where supply keeps pace with demand, reducing input-cost pressures and predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The bivariate combination of INDPRO and RSAFS fails to produce a positive and statistically significant marginal effect (p > 0.05) on 20-day forward returns relative to single-variable models over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0% and increases month-over-month, restrictive monetary policy elevates discount rates and debt-service costs, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not negative in at least 55% of instances when the trigger fires over any 3-year rolling window.
